title: Play It Again, Figrin D'an: The Tale of Muftak and Kabe

"'''Play It Again, Figrin D'an: The Tale of Muftak and Kabe'''" is a short story written by [[A. C. Crispin]] from ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]'', which was published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[August]] [[1995]]. The story, about [[Muftak/Legends|Muftak]] and [[Kabe/Legends|Kabe]], takes place in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].

==Plot summary==
[[Muftak/Legends|Muftak]] has long wondered about his origins, so when a passing [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] identifies his [[Species/Legends|species]] as [[Talz/Legends|Talz]], the large furry biped's curiosity is piqued. Wanting to explore the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] beyond [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], he agrees to his friend [[Kabe/Legends|Kabe's]] long repeated request to rob [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Desilijic Complex/Legends|town house]]. The two ragamuffins infiltrate the building, where they find a [[Prisoner/Legends|captive]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] who promises them [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] if they deliver a [[Datadot]] containing valuable [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Rebel intelligence]]. When security teams surround Muftak and Kabe and a house [[Fire/Legends|fire]] breaks out in the resulting gunfight, the two urchins leave the blaze without any riches, but pass on the datadot to a second Rebel agent. This secures them thousands of credits and passage off-world, allowing them to leave their lowly station.
